notes:
DISCOVERY MODULE — sparse Set<string> storage keyed by `${gx}_${gy}`. Tile size = 32 world px. revealArea iterates only the radius's grid footprint (not the full map) and uses circular distance check, so it's cheap to call every frame.

PERSISTED PAYLOAD SHAPE (canonical):
  {
    "tiles":  ["3_5", "3_6", "4_5", ...],   // sparse list of revealed grid cells
    "width":  1024,                            // world map width in px (at init)
    "height": 768,                             // world map height in px (at init)
    "tile":   32                               // DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE — embedded for forward compat
  }

SCHEMA VERSION: NOT bumped. Stays at v4. Rationale: the change is purely additive (a new top-level `discovery` field), and the existing `loadGame` logic already does `{ ...defaultSave(), ...migrated }` which fills missing fields from defaults. Old saves without a `discovery` field load with the empty default `{ tiles: [], width: 0, height: 0, tile: 32 }`, then loadDiscoveryState() resolves that to an empty Set. No migration function needed.

BACKWARDS-COMPAT VERIFICATION: Ran a node integration test stubbing localStorage with an old-shape v4 save lacking the `discovery` field entirely. Results:
  - loaded.discovery present, with .tiles=[]
  - _debugGetRevealedSet().size === 0 — module hydrated to empty Set
  - Unrelated fields (player.x, etc.) preserved
  - Round-trip: revealed tiles via revealArea, called saveGame(), wiped state, reloaded — discovered tiles came back, isDiscovered(300,300) still true.
Also unit-tested the discovery module in isolation: empty-on-init, reveal-then-query, snapshot-and-rehydrate, null/undefined/{} payloads → empty set, non-string entries filtered out. ALL TESTS PASSED.

MEMORY BUDGET: For a 1024×768 world map at DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE=32, the grid is 32×24 = 768 cells. A fully-discovered map serializes to ~5.6 KB JSON (measured: 5631 bytes). Even at 4× the resolution (e.g. a 4096×3072 world), full discovery is ~12,288 cells ≈ ~90 KB JSON — comfortably within reason. In practice the world will be far from fully discovered, so typical save overhead is well under 1 KB.

SAVESYSTEM INTEGRATION:
  - Added `import { getDiscoveryState, loadDiscoveryState } from './discoverySystem.js';`
  - Added `discovery: { tiles: [], width: 0, height: 0, tile: 32 }` to defaultSave().
  - In loadGame(): after the defaults-merge, calls loadDiscoveryState(merged.discovery) inside a try/catch so a bad payload never breaks the load.
  - In saveGame(): pulls live state via getDiscoveryState() and overwrites state.discovery before persisting — so callers don't have to remember to copy the discovery payload into their game-state object before saving. Falls back to state.discovery (then to empty default) if the module call throws.

SCOPE COMPLIANCE: Touched ONLY src/renderer/game/systems/discoverySystem.js (new) and src/renderer/game/systems/saveSystem.js (additive). Did not modify any scene file (WorldMapScene.js was already dirty pre-run from the parallel agent's prior baseline / unrelated edits — confirmed via git status comparison against the agent spec's stated baseline). Did not touch src/renderer/game3d/. Did not bump schema version. No new top-level dependencies. JavaScript only — no TypeScript.
